name: AI Cheese Grading
purpose: Deploy computer vision AI to automate visual quality inspection of cheese wheels during aging — replace manual sampling
current_situation: Quality grading is 100% manual — trained experts inspect each wheel visually and by touch. Throughput limited to 200 wheels/day per expert.
expected_benefits: Increase inspection throughput 5x, reduce quality escapes by 40%, and free expert time for complex cases. ROI estimated at 18 months.
risks: AI model accuracy on artisanal cheese is unproven — surface variations are extreme. Risk of false positives rejecting good wheels. Need 6+ months of training data.

name: Smart Packaging Automation
purpose: Automate cheese packaging lines with robotic arms and weight-based sorting — reduce manual handling and improve consistency
current_situation: Packaging is semi-manual with 25 operators across 3 sites. High variability in pack weights and frequent labeling errors.
expected_benefits: Reduce packaging labor by 60%, eliminate weight variance, and improve traceability with inline QR coding. Payback in 24 months.
risks: Significant CAPEX (€800K+). Integration with existing conveyor systems at Parma site is complex. Union consultation required for FR staff.

name: Cave Climate Digital Twin
purpose: Create a digital twin of all cheese aging caves — model airflow, temperature gradients and microbial activity for optimal aging
current_situation: CaveGuard provides real-time monitoring but no predictive modeling. Cave masters rely on decades of experience to adjust conditions.
expected_benefits: Predict optimal turning schedules, reduce aging defects by 30%, and enable remote cave management. Could save €200K/year in product loss.
risks: Requires extensive sensor data history (12+ months from CaveGuard). Physics modeling of 15th-century caves is challenging. Key-person risk on cave master expertise.

name: Automated Inventory Counting
purpose: RFID-based automated inventory counting for cheese warehouses — replace quarterly manual stock takes
current_situation: Quarterly physical inventory takes 3 days per site with 8 people. Accuracy is 94% — discrepancies cause order fulfillment issues.
expected_benefits: Real-time inventory accuracy above 99%, eliminate manual counting labor (€150K/year savings), and enable dynamic reorder points.
risks: On hold pending SAP Cheddar migration — inventory module architecture will change. RFID tag cost per wheel (€0.15) adds up at 2M wheels/year.
